mnemonics: K5 and K3-3 are the only non-planar culprits. Remember them as the 'Forbidden Pair'.
keyPoints: A finite graph is planar if and only if it does not contain a subgraph that is a subdivision of K5 or K3,3.
K5 is the complete graph on 5 vertices.
K3,3 is the complete bipartite graph with 3 vertices in each partition.
commonMistakes: Assuming a graph is non-planar only if K5 or K3,3 is present as a subgraph (it must be a subdivision/homeomorphic copy).
Forgetting that the theorem is an 'if and only if' statement, meaning these are the ONLY two fundamental non-planar graphs.
